Farn Porrrrcar Pna.crIcps C ouvttssloN
P.O. Box 807 . -128 J Street. Sacramento. CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
May 23,2001,
Deborah S. Acker, Councilmember
City of Ontario
2836F,. Big Range Road
Ontario, CA9l76l
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-117
Dear Ms. Acker
Re
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Fottical Reform Act (the "Act").l You are requesting follow
up advice regarding the Acker Advice Letter, No. A-01-055. Please note that we do not
provide advice relating to past conduct. @eg. i8329(b)(8).)
QUESTIONS
1. As a member of the Ontario City Council, may you write newspaper articles
regarding a matter in which you may have a conflict of interest?
2. As a member of the Ontario City Council, may you appear as a guest speaker
on a radio program to discuss a matter in which you may have a conflict of interest?
CONCLUSION
Yes. The conflict-of-interest provisions do not prohibit you from expressing your
opinion to the public or the press by writing a newspaper article or appearing on a radio
program.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
1 8 1 0 9- 1 8997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-01-117
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
Since you are a public oflicial, Section 87100 of the Act prohibits you from
making, participating in making, or otherwise attempting to use your official position to
influence a governmental decision in which you have a financial interest.
When a decision is before your own agency, you are considered to be using your
offrcial position to influence the decision when you contact or appear before any member,
officer, employee or consultant of your agency regarding the decision. (Reg.
18702.3(a).)
For decisions that are before other agencies that are not controlled by your
agency, you are considered to be using your official position to influence the decision if
you act or purport to act as a representative of your agency. (Reg. 18702.3(b).) For
example, if you write a letter to another city on your oflicial city council stationery to
persuade that city to make a certain governmental decision, you will be "attempting to
use your official position to influence a governmental decision."
"Influencing" does not include communicating with the public or the press. (R"g
18702.4(b).) Thus, we have advised that an official who has a conflict of interest in a
given decision may still communicate his or her opinion about the decision at a public
meetirig or forum where city ofiicials are not present. (Gallagher Advice Letter, No. I-
94-279.) In this case, expressing your opinion by writing newspaper articles. or appearing
as a guest speaker on a radio program also qualify as communicating with the public or
the press. As such, the prohibition in Section 87100 does not prohibit you from doing
these activities even if you have a conflict of interest in the subject matter of the
communications.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Julia Bilaver
StaffCounsel

sections.question:
1. As a member of the Ontario City Council, may you write newspaper articles
regarding a matter in which you may have a conflict of interest?
2. As a member of the Ontario City Council, may you appear as a guest speaker
on a radio program to discuss a matter in which you may have a conflict of interest?
sections.conclusion:
Yes. The conflict-of-interest provisions do not prohibit you from expressing your
opinion to the public or the press by writing a newspaper article or appearing on a radio
program.
